brief_summary: FastAPI is a composition of two primary libraries. At a high level, FastAPI is a high-performance Python web framework for building APIs, primarily composed of Starlette. The main stack includes Starlette, Pydantic. Documented public surfaces include OpenAPI, JSON Schema, Swagger UI, ReDoc. The documented workflow emphasizes A `fastapi` CLI uses Uvicorn for a live-reloading development server.

detailed_summary.heading: Architecture
bullets: FastAPI is a composition of two primary libraries.
Starlette: Provides all core web and ASGI (Asynchronous Server Gateway Interface) functionality, including native support for WebSockets, CORS, and Cookie Sessions.
Pydantic: Handles all data validation, serialization/deserialization, and type casting.
It adheres to open standards, using OpenAPI for API specification and JSON Schema for data models.

detailed_summary.heading: APIs & Features
bullets: API endpoints, parameters (path, query, header, cookie), and bodies are declared using Python type hints in function signatures, supporting both `def` and `async def`.
Generates interactive API documentation, providing Swagger UI (at `/docs`) and ReDoc (at `/redoc`) by default.
Pydantic automatically validates incoming data (including nested JSON) and serializes outgoing data, supporting standard Python types plus `datetime`, `UUID`, etc.
A built-in dependency injection system manages resources, authentication, and shared logic like database connections.
Includes helpers for security schemes like OAuth2 (with JWT tokens) and HTTP Basic.
Supports GraphQL with libraries like Strawberry.
A `fastapi` CLI uses Uvicorn for a live-reloading development server.
The `fastapi[standard]` installation bundles `uvicorn`, `httpx` (for `TestClient`), `python-multipart`, `email-validator`, and `jinja2`.
Optional JSON encoders like `orjson` and `ujson` are supported.
The creator also developed Typer, a sibling project for building command-line applications based on the same principles.

detailed_summary.heading: Maturity
bullets: Used in production by Microsoft, Uber, Netflix, and Cisco.
TechEmpower benchmarks consistently rank FastAPI (on Uvicorn) as one of the fastest Python frameworks, with performance claimed to be on par with NodeJS and Go.
It is typically behind only its core components, Starlette and Uvicorn, in Python benchmarks.
The project is actively maintained with frequent releases and CI workflows.
The security policy officially supports only the latest version.
It is primarily sponsored by FastAPI Cloud, a commercial platform from the same team.

detailed_summary.heading: Notable Issues & Considerations
bullets: Complex, recurring bugs exist around resolving `ForwardRef` and `Annotated` type hints, especially with `from __future__ import annotations`, which can cause misclassified parameters or incorrect OpenAPI schemas.
Long-standing requests include official support for class-based views (CBVs) and a mechanism to apply authentication to static files.
The dependency injection system's complexity has led to deadlocks when managing limited resource pools (e.g., database connections), though specific instances have been fixed.
Edge cases can produce incorrect OpenAPI schemas, such as duplicate `operationId` when a single route is registered with multiple HTTP methods.
While fast, performance regressions have occurred, such as a memory usage spike linked to the `Dependant` object.

Type Hint Resolution: Complex, recurring bugs exist around resolving `ForwardRef` and `Annotated` type hints, especially with `from __future__ import annotations`, which can cause misclassified parameters or incorrect OpenAPI schemas (Issues #15364, #15307, #15300).
Feature Requests: Long-standing requests include official support for class-based views (CBVs) (#15392, #2625) and a mechanism to apply authentication to static files (#15295, #858).
Concurrency Model: The dependency injection system's complexity has led to deadlocks when managing limited resource pools (e.g., database connections), though specific instances have been fixed (#15388).
Schema Generation: Edge cases can produce incorrect OpenAPI schemas, such as duplicate `operationId` when a single route is registered with multiple HTTP methods (#15398).
Performance Regressions: While fast, performance regressions have occurred, such as a memory usage spike linked to the `Dependant` object (#15336).

structured_payload.architecture_overview: FastAPI is a high-performance Python web framework for building APIs, primarily composed of Starlette for core web and ASGI functionality, and Pydantic for robust data validation, serialization, and type casting. It leverages standard Python type hints for API declaration and adheres to open standards like OpenAPI for API specification and JSON Schema for data models, enhancing developer experience and ensuring strong editor support.

benchmarks_tests_examples: Internal tests claim a 200-300% increase in development speed and a 40% reduction in human-induced errors.
TechEmpower benchmarks consistently rank FastAPI (on Uvicorn) as one of the fastest Python frameworks.
